tool.name: GaCDI Manifest Builder (IDC)
tool.description: build a filtered IDC imaging manifest (GCS, one row per DICOM series) and metadata
tool.command: gacdi-manifest idc #if str($core.collection_id).strip() != '' --collection-id '$core.collection_id' #end if #if str($core.patient_id).strip() != '' --patient-id '$core.patient_id' #end if #for $mf in $adv.metadata_filters --metadata-filter 'column=${mf.column};op=${mf.op};values=${mf.values}' #end for #if $count_only --count-only #else #if str($adv.max_files) != '' --max-files $adv.max_files #end if #end if --manifest-out '$manifest' --metadata-out '$metadata' --report-out '$report' --selection-manifest-out '$selection_manifest' --selection-metadata-out '$selection_metadata' --selection-provenance-out '$selection_provenance'

param.name: collection_id
param.label: IDC collection id(s)
param.help: One or more IDC collection ids (comma-separated), e.g. 4d_lung, tcga_luad. Browse collections at https://portal.imaging.datacommons.cancer.gov/.

param.name: patient_id
param.label: Patient id(s)
param.help: Optional. One or more PatientIDs (comma-separated) to restrict the manifest to a specific cohort.

param.name: count_only
param.label: Preview counts only (don't build the manifest yet)
param.help: Reports how many DICOM instances match so you can gauge size before building the (series-level) manifest.

repeat.name: metadata_filters
repeat.title: Filter the final table by metadata column
repeat.help: Applied AFTER the query, on the metadata columns this tool generated (e.g. project, case_id, or a native column like idc__StudyInstanceUID). Keeps only rows where a column is present or matches specific value(s).

param.name: column
param.label: Metadata column
param.help: Exact column header from the metadata output, e.g. case_id, project, idc__PatientID.

param.name: op
param.label: Condition
param.help: How to test each row's value.

param.name: values
param.label: Value(s)
param.help: Comma-separated. Required for 'is one of', 'is not', and 'contains'; leave blank for 'is present' / 'is blank'.

param.name: max_files
param.label: Maximum number of series
param.help: Stop after this many series — handy for a quick test on a large collection. Leave blank for all matches.

tool.help:
**GaCDI Manifest Builder (IDC)**
Builds a **targeted** download manifest from the NCI `Imaging Data Commons <https://imaging.datacommons.cancer.gov/>`_ (IDC), a CRDC node, so you fetch exactly the DICOM imaging series you want. It produces three compatibility outputs plus the canonical three-file selection bundle:
1. **IDC GCS manifest** — the multi-source download contract, one row **per DICOM series**. Each row carries ``download_method=gcs`` and ``access_url = gs://idc-open-data/<series-uuid>/`` (the public IDC bucket), plus ``file_format=DICOM`` and ``case_id`` = PatientID. 2. **metadata** — a harmonized core (``project`` = collection, ``case_id`` = PatientID …) plus every native IDC field preserved as ``idc__`` prefixed columns. 3. **report** — match counts and provenance; in preview mode, the matching instance count. 4. **canonical selection manifest / metadata / provenance** — the validated, hash-bound source-neutral bundle reserved for the future IDC GCS downloader.
**Getting started**
Give an **IDC collection id** (e.g. ``4d_lung``) — optionally narrowed by *Patient id(s)* — and run with *Preview counts only* first (it reports matching DICOM **instances**; the built manifest has one row per **series**). When the count looks right, turn preview off. For a large collection use *Maximum number of series* to cap a quick test.
